title: Iskandar (Rider, Fate/Zero)
content:
Iskandar, also known as Alexander the Great, is the Rider-class Servant of Waver Velvet in the Fourth Holy Grail War as depicted in Fate/Zero. He is widely considered one of the most charismatic and powerful Servants in the war, beloved for his boisterous personality and genuine warmth.

Iskandar embodies the ideal of conquest through camaraderie and shared vision rather than subjugation. He sought to unite the world under his banner not through force alone but by inspiring loyalty and admiration — in life, his soldiers followed him to the ends of the earth out of love.

His Noble Phantasms reflect this philosophy:

1. **Via Expugnatio: Distant Trampling Domination** — A powerful physical charge using his divine horse Bucephalus, which can destroy fortifications and crush enemies.

2. **Ionioi Hetairoi: Army of the King** — His Reality Marble, which manifests the entire spirit world of his loyal companions from life. Ten thousand soldiers who swore absolute loyalty to him in life are summoned as Heroic Spirits themselves, creating an overwhelming army. This Noble Phantasm is his trump card and the culmination of his legend as the king who conquered the known world through brotherhood.

Waver Velvet, a student at the Clock Tower who stole his grandfather's catalyst to participate in the war, serves as Iskandar's Master. Despite initially being unimpressive, Waver grows significantly under Iskandar's mentorship, developing courage and confidence. Iskandar genuinely comes to view Waver as a worthy vassal.

Iskandar's clash of ideologies with Gilgamesh (Archer) is a recurring theme — Gilgamesh recognizes Iskandar as the only king worthy of his respect, though he disagrees entirely with his philosophy. Their conversations about kingship are considered among the most philosophical moments in the Fate franchise.

Iskandar is ultimately defeated by Gilgamesh in their final battle. He asks Gilgamesh whether he has any regrets before dying — and Gilgamesh, for the first time, acknowledges him as the one man who might have been his equal.

title: Lancelot (Berserker, Fate/Zero)
content:
Lancelot du Lac, the Knight of the Lake, is summoned as the Berserker-class Servant of Kariya Matou in the Fourth Holy Grail War. His true identity is kept secret for most of Fate/Zero, adding to the mystery surrounding this silent, mask-wearing black knight.

Lancelot chose the Berserker class — despite being capable of serving in Saber — out of guilt. He believed he deserved punishment for betraying his king, Artoria (Saber). By placing himself under the Mad Enhancement of Berserker, he sacrificed his sanity and clarity in penance for the role his affair with Guinevere played in the fall of Camelot.

His Noble Phantasm is Knight of Owner: A Knight Does Not Die with Empty Hands. This ability allows him to designate any object he touches as his Noble Phantasm, endowing it with equivalent power to his true Noble Phantasms. Combined with his exceptional skill, he can take a fighter jet and use it as effectively as his legendary sword Arondight. This makes him extraordinarily versatile and unpredictable.

His class skill For Someone's Glory allows him to conceal his true name, taking on a black knight appearance and preventing recognition.

Kariya Matou is one of the most tragic Masters in Fate/Zero. He returns to the Matou family (which he had abandoned) and undergoes painful Crest Worm augmentation to gain enough magical energy to participate in the war, solely to save Sakura Matou from Zouken. His body deteriorates throughout the war due to the worms consuming him.

When Lancelot's identity is finally revealed to Saber, she is devastated — she blames herself for Lancelot's fall and the collapse of her Round Table. Lancelot, in a moment of clarity before death, reveals that he never blamed her and wished only that she had punished him for his sin.
